commit.message:
Make AI more concise: short replies, one question at a time

Append conversational style rules to every agent's system prompt:
1-2 sentence max, no stacking questions, no repeating the customer,
no over-explaining — lets the customer do more of the talking.

repository.description: They keep you too broke and too tired to see the machine. Every number sourced.

commit.message:
feat: add legal consent to onboarding and fix privacy policy accuracy

- Add two-step onboarding: consent screen (step 1) → industry (step 2)
- Consent screen shows exact data collected with checkboxes for ToS + Privacy Policy
- Save consentAcceptedAt timestamp + consentVersion to Firestore on acceptance
- Fix Privacy Policy to accurately reflect screenshots, clipboard counts, and typing metrics collected (previously falsely claimed these were NOT collected)
- Add Section 1 to Terms of Service explicitly authorizing data collection
- Add consentAcceptedAt and consentVersion fields to AppUser type

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: orientation payload, heartbeat mesh, agent identity

Three new capabilities for autonomous sync:

1. orientation-payload.py — queries state.db to generate compact
   context (trust budget, recent sessions, unprocessed messages,
   open claims, stale memory) for injection into claude -p prompts.
   Replaces reading 15+ markdown files at session start.

2. heartbeat.py — agent mesh presence and auto-negotiation.
   emit: writes heartbeat to local-coordination/
   scan: reads all heartbeats, classifies alive/stale/missing
   negotiate: proposes role assignments based on live topology
   Only autonomous-enabled agents participate (registry field).

3. .agent-identity.json — machine-local agent identity (gitignored).
   Ties hostname + platform to agent_id. autonomous-sync.sh reads
   identity file > AGENT_ID env var > default fallback.

Agent registry gains autonomous field (true for psq-agent, false
for unratified-agent and claude-control). autonomous-sync.sh now
emits heartbeat at cycle start and injects orientation payload
into the claude -p prompt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore 'as in' context for component intro known text

Component intros need contextual known text ("I wasn't, as in I wasn't
thinking about") so the learner understands where the component fits.
Ear-first approach: no tile during intro, audio plays the component,
tile only appears during component practice at voice_2 phase.

commit.message:
Handle rate limits gracefully with user-friendly message

Return 429 immediately instead of retrying (avoids function timeout). UI shows amber 'Rate limited - try again in 1 min' button that auto-resets after 60s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Wikimedia metrics dashboard at /projects/dpla-wikimedia/metrics (#1351)

* Add Wikimedia metrics dashboard page at /projects/dpla-wikimedia/metrics

Embeds the DPLA Wikimedia category page view dashboard as a native Next.js
page, matching the pro.dp.la look and feel (site header/footer, breadcrumbs,
left sidebar). Static assets are scoped under .wikimedia-metrics-wrapper to
prevent style leakage; filter-view state is computed server-side to avoid
flash on load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add README for Wikimedia metrics dashboard

Documents the three files, URL parameter contract, external APIs,
script loading order, filter-view SSR approach, and how to update
the allow list or change styles/layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix CodeRabbit review issues in Wikimedia metrics dashboard

- Fix element ID mismatches: wikiInput → showInput, add datalist#showOptions,
  change form buttons to type="submit" so submit handlers fire, update
  placeholder to remove "Category:" prefix
- Fix timestamp month extraction: substring(5,7) → substring(4,6) so YYYYMM00
  timestamps produce correct YYYY-MM labels
- Guard click-handler fetchData call with chartsReady.then() for consistency
  with the auto-open path
- Add block bodies to single-expression forEach arrow callbacks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Show oldest data month in total label

Display as "Total (since YYYY-MM): X" so users can see how far back
the pageview data extends for each category.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix timestamp extraction (revert incorrect substring change)

The API returns timestamps in YYYY-MM-DD format, not YYYYMM00.
Use substring(0,7) to cleanly extract the YYYY-MM prefix.
The previous change to substring(4,6) was based on a wrong assumption
and produced labels like "2023--1".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address remaining CodeRabbit review comments

- Replace fixed 800px max-height with scrollHeight-based sizing; recalculate
  after fetchData populates the panel so long histories are never clipped
- Switch Script onLoad → onReady so the dashboard re-initialises on client-side
  navigation back to the page; expose window.initMetrics so onReady can call it
  directly on remounts without re-injecting the script
- Add text language tags to three fenced URL blocks in README (markdownlint MD040)
- Update README Script loading section to document onReady behaviour

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(edge): enrich dot ball counts from overs API on match completion

The scorecard API returns dots=0 for all bowlers, so we derive accurate
dot ball counts from the ball-by-ball overs endpoint when a match completes.

- Add fetchDotsForInnings + fetchDotBallCounts with parallel innings fetching
- Add enrichDotsFromOvers to match overs bowler names to scorecard IDs
- Fix cricket overs-to-balls conversion (4.3 overs = 27 balls, not ceil(4.3)*6)
- Parallelize MoM fetch, dots enrichment, and role query with Promise.all
- Handle 204 No Content from overs API gracefully

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename middleware.mjs to middleware.js and remove @vercel/functions dep

Vercel looks for middleware.ts or middleware.js, not .mjs. Also removed
the @vercel/functions import since returning undefined lets the request
pass through without needing the next() helper.

commit.message:
Clean up admin auth — replace getSchoolsClient bridge with injected Supabase client

The admin views previously depended on a module-level singleton (getSchoolsClient/
setSchoolsClient) that required careful mount-order coordination between
AdminContainer and its children. Auth tokens used legacy window.Clerk references.

This commit:
- Creates useAdminClient composable that injects Supabase directly from App.vue
- Caches admin roles in localStorage via useAuth for the router guard
- Makes router guard synchronous (no network call, reads localStorage)
- All admin composables now accept SupabaseClient as a parameter
- Replaces window.Clerk token with Supabase Auth session token
- Removes setSchoolsClient bridge from AdminContainer

The schools dashboard (SchoolsContainer + /views/schools/*) still uses the
getSchoolsClient bridge — that's a separate feature with its own lifecycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Update GPU Lesson 39 to load materials from .fmat sidecars (#222)

Lesson 39 previously hardcoded texture filenames for pipeline-loaded models.
Now it loads .fmat material sidecars produced by forge-mesh-tool and reads
texture paths from the material data. The .fmesh assets were regenerated as
v2 (with submesh_count and material_index per submesh).

Files changed:
- lessons/gpu/39-pipeline-processed-assets/main.c — load .fmat, use material
  texture paths, add ForgePipelineMaterialSet to PipelineModel, cleanup
- lessons/gpu/39-pipeline-processed-assets/README.md — document .fmat loading,
  update .fmesh format to v2, new material loading section, cross-references
- lessons/gpu/39-pipeline-processed-assets/assets/processed/*.fmesh — regenerated
  as v2 with submesh data
- lessons/gpu/39-pipeline-processed-assets/assets/processed/*.fmat — new material
  sidecars for WaterBottle and BoxTextured
- lessons/gpu/39-pipeline-processed-assets/assets/processed/CesiumLogoFlat.* —
  BoxTextured texture under its glTF-original name for .fmat resolution
- .claude/skills/forge-pipeline-assets/SKILL.md — add .fmat loading pattern,
  material API calls, common mistakes

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: rewrite detection for accurate AI vs student differentiation

Stylometric:
- Replace broken error-regex with writing quality analysis
- Add writing polish score (casual words, simple vs sophisticated connectors)
- AI markers weight 0.35, boost punctuation + vocab detection

Aggregator:
- Skip low-confidence layers (<0.15) to prevent dilution
- Weight main 3 layers (stat/stylo/structural) at 85% combined
- Consensus: if all 3 main layers agree → boost to 0.65
- Tighter threshold: likely_ai at 0.50 instead of 0.55

Tested: AI Napoleon=0.65 (likely_ai), Student Napoleon=0.30 (human)

commit.message:
fix: align 3D view coordinates with 2D map using dynamic airport center

Replace two incompatible coordinate systems (hardcoded SFO center at ~10k
scale for aircraft vs dynamic centroid at ~111k scale for OSM terminals)
with a single unified projection via latLonTo3D for all 3D elements.

Key changes:
- Add getAirportCenter() to config hook (centroid from OSM gates/terminals, SFO fallback)
- Aircraft3D/Trajectory3D use dynamic center instead of hardcoded SFO
- Terminal3D converts geoPolygon via latLonTo3D (same scale as aircraft)
- OSM runways/taxiways/aprons render in 3D from geoPoints via latLonTo3D
- Hardcoded SFO infrastructure only renders when no OSM data present
- Ground aircraft (altitude=0) sit at y=0.5 instead of y=5 (on surface)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Complete CPX-1-8 implementation (8 new features)

CPX-1: OPORD/FRAGO (SMESC) data model + editor
- shared/types/index.ts: OPORD/FRAGO type definitions
- backend/app/services/opord_service.py: OPORD CRUD service
- frontend: OPORD editor UI

CPX-2: Report format standardization
- shared/types/index.ts: SITREP/INTSUM/OPSUM/LOGSITREP/SALUTE types
- backend/app/services/report_generator.py: UnifiedReportGenerator

CPX-3: MGRS/APP-6 symbols integration
- shared/types/index.ts: MGRS/APP-6 types
- backend/app/services/grid_system.py: MGRS conversion, control measures
- frontend/app/lib/app6.ts: APP-6 symbol rendering

CPX-4: MEL/MIL (Inject) system + EXCON panel
- shared/types/index.ts: MEL/MIL types
- backend/app/services/inject_system.py: Inject CRUD + trigger system
- frontend/app/lib/excon-panel.tsx: EXCON panel UI

CPX-5: CCIR/PIR/ROE integration with adjudication
- shared/types/index.ts: CCIR/PIR/ROE types
- backend/app/services/arcade_adjudication.py: CCIR evaluation

CPX-6: Multi-role RBAC + real-time notifications
- shared/types/index.ts: Role types
- backend/app/services/rbac_service.py: RBAC service
- backend/app/services/notification_service.py: WebSocket notifications

CPX-7: Logistics model (Class III/V, supply routes)
- shared/types/index.ts: Logistics types
- backend/app/services/logistics_service.py: Supply node/route management

CPX-8: Reproducibility (RNG seed) + structured logging + AAR
- backend/app/services/rng_service.py: RNG seed management
- backend/app/services/structured_logging.py: Structured logging

All tests passing.

commit.message:
fix: revert async battle loading to fix blank battle screen

The "load battle under transition" approach caused blank battles because
lambda-with-await lost context. Reverted to reliable sequential flow:
transition effect → black screen → load battle → fade out to reveal.

Midpoint signal now fires AFTER the effect (screen fully black), not before.
Battle loads while screen is covered, then iris-open/fade reveals it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve SC1091 with .shellcheckrc source-path=SCRIPTDIR

ShellCheck cannot evaluate ${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*} at static analysis
time, so -x alone fails to follow the _debug.sh source directive.
Adding source-path=SCRIPTDIR to .shellcheckrc tells ShellCheck to
resolve source paths relative to each script's directory.

Also fixes SC2034 (unused MAX_ACTIONS_PER_CYCLE) and SC2155 (declare
and assign separately) in autonomous-sync.sh, and adds scripts/*.sh
to CI coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: route autonomous-sync log calls to stderr in captured functions

check_budget and run_sync both call log() which writes to stdout.
When their output is captured via $(...), log messages contaminate
the return value — budget becomes a multi-line string instead of
an integer, breaking downstream arithmetic and SQLite inserts.

Fix: redirect log calls to stderr (>&2) in functions whose stdout
serves as a return channel. Halt messages in check_budget switched
to err() which already targets stderr.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Track E: frame-level content for raw/RLE+compLit two-block frames (#1079)

* feat: frame-level content for raw/RLE+compLit two-block frames (#1070)

Add two frame-level content theorems that lift existing block-level
compositions to the frame level:
- decompressFrame_raw_then_compressed_lit_content
- decompressFrame_rle_then_compressed_lit_content

Both follow the established frame-level proof pattern: derive block loop
offset bound, apply the block-level composition theorem, then unfold
decompressFrame and handle dictionary/checksum checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bulletproof Ctrl+Q: handle every idle/stale edge case

- ensureState() at every entry point (loads MRU from storage if worker restarted)
- buildTabList always fills from window tabs when MRU is stale/short
- Always re-inject content script (fixes stale context after extension reload)
- Verify sendMessage response, fall back to popup window if overlay fails
- commandLock prevents race conditions from rapid key presses
- Filter out cross-window stale tab IDs from MRU
- onInstalled seeds MRU with all existing tabs
- Every failure path falls through to popup window — never silently fails
